parentQuestion: A patient cannot detect vibration from a tuning fork placed on their ankle but can still detect it on their knee. Light touch is intact at both locations. Which receptor type is selectively impaired?

scaffolds.q: Do different mechanoreceptors detect different types of touch?
options: No, all touch receptors are identical
Yes, different receptors detect light touch vs vibration
Only one type of touch exists
scaffolds.explain: Light touch is detected by MEISSNER'S CORPUSCLES and MERKEL DISCS. Vibration is detected by PACINIAN CORPUSCLES. Different receptors, different jobs.

wrongExplains.0: Touch receptors are specialized. Each type detects specific stimuli.
wrongExplains.2: Mechanoreceptors definitely exist — they detect all mechanical stimuli.
wrongExplains.3: Many types of touch exist: Light, deep pressure, vibration, etc.

scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES are deep, encapsulated receptors that respond to HIGH-FREQUENCY VIBRATION (40-400 Hz). A tuning fork test specifically targets them.

wrongExplains.0: Temperature is detected by thermoreceptors. Pacinian detect vibration.
wrongExplains.2: Pain is detected by nociceptors. Pacinian detect vibration.
wrongExplains.3: Light touch is detected by Meissner's and Merkel's. Pacinian detect vibration.

scaffolds.q: What do Meissner's corpuscles and Merkel discs detect?
scaffolds.explain: These SUPERFICIAL receptors detect LIGHT TOUCH and texture. They're sensitive to gentle pressure on the skin surface.

wrongExplains.0: Vibration is detected by Pacinian corpuscles. These detect light touch.
wrongExplains.2: Deep pressure activates Pacinian. Meissner's and Merkel's are superficial.
wrongExplains.3: Pain is detected by nociceptors. These detect gentle touch.

scaffolds.q: Can vibration and light touch be affected separately?
options: Yes, because they use different receptors
Neither can be affected by disease
scaffolds.explain: Disease can damage ONE type of receptor or pathway while SPARING others. This is called DISSOCIATED SENSORY LOSS — selective damage.

wrongExplains.0: Different receptors can be selectively damaged by disease.
wrongExplains.2: Touch modalities use different receptors and can be separately affected.
wrongExplains.3: Disease definitely affects touch sensation, sometimes selectively.

scaffolds.q: In early neuropathy, does vibration or light touch fail first?
options: Vibration sense often fails before light touch
scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES (vibration) may be more vulnerable than light touch receptors. VIBRATION LOSS is an early sign of nerve damage.

wrongExplains.0: Vibration often fails first. Light touch may be preserved longer.
wrongExplains.2: They can fail at different rates due to receptor vulnerability differences.
wrongExplains.3: Neuropathy definitely causes sensory loss, often vibration first.

scaffolds.q: Why is testing vibration at different locations useful?
options: It maps the progression of nerve damage
scaffolds.explain: Lost at ankle but present at knee? The damage reaches UP TO ankle level. A tuning fork becomes a DIAGNOSTIC MAPPING TOOL for neuropathy.

wrongExplains.0: Location testing is very useful for mapping nerve damage.
wrongExplains.2: Tuning forks on skin test vibration sense, not hearing.
wrongExplains.3: Blood pressure uses a cuff. Vibration tests use a tuning fork.

scaffolds.q: What does preserved light touch but lost vibration indicate?
scaffolds.explain: Different receptors, different fates. PACINIAN CORPUSCLES are damaged while LIGHT TOUCH RECEPTORS survive. This is selective receptor damage.

wrongExplains.0: The preserved light touch shows receptors are NOT equally damaged.
wrongExplains.2: Lost vibration sense indicates pathology, not normalcy.
wrongExplains.3: This is sensory receptor damage, not motor.

scaffolds.q: What does selective loss of vibration with preserved light touch indicate?
scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES detect vibration. When they fail, VIBRATION SENSE is lost while LIGHT TOUCH (different receptors) remains intact.

wrongExplains.0: Meissner's damage would affect light touch. Light touch is preserved here.
wrongExplains.2: Complete transection would affect ALL sensations. This is selective.
wrongExplains.3: Brain damage would cause different patterns. This is receptor-level.

scaffolds.q: Which receptor type is selectively impaired in this patient?
scaffolds.explain: PACINIAN CORPUSCLES! These detect vibration. Lost vibration + preserved light touch = SELECTIVE PACINIAN DAMAGE.

wrongExplains.0: Free nerve endings detect pain. This patient has vibration loss.
wrongExplains.1: Meissner's corpuscles detect light touch, which is preserved.
wrongExplains.3: Merkel discs detect sustained touch, which is preserved.
